_comment: Example genesis config for the attestation module. Operators copy this,
fill in real values, and pass it to the chain at devnet launch.
Address and pubkey fields are 32-byte arrays. The format below is the
default serde representation; a future PR can add hex serialisation.
treasury: address that receives the treasury share of every attestation
fee plus the full amount of registration fees.
*_fee fields: $LGT amounts in nanos (9 decimals). 1_000_000 nanos = 0.001 $LGT.
9 decimals matches Solana's lamport convention; gives 18.4x headroom over a 1B
supply cap inside u64 (sov-bank's amount type) while leaving 1000x more fee-
pricing granularity than the 6-decimal alternative would.
Defaults:
attestation_fee:           1_000_000          (0.001 $LGT)
schema_registration_fee:   100_000_000_000    (100 $LGT)
attestor_set_fee:          10_000_000_000     (10 $LGT)
initial_attestor_sets: registered at genesis with the same rules as
RegisterAttestorSet. Stored members are sorted before hashing.
initial_schemas: registered at genesis. Each schema's `attestor_set` must
reference an AttestorSetId derived from one of the initial_attestor_sets
above (operators precompute this with attestation::AttestorSet::derive_id).
fee_routing_addr must be Some iff fee_routing_bps > 0; cap is 5000 (50%).

initial_attestor_sets._comment: Themisra's launch attestor set. 2-of-3 threshold over three Ligate-operated nodes; replace with real attestor pubkeys before launch.

initial_schemas._comment: Themisra Proof-of-Prompt v1. attestor_set is the AttestorSetId derived from the set above; precompute with attestation::AttestorSet::derive_id(&members, threshold).
initial_schemas.name: themisra.proof-of-prompt
